question_title: Will Taipower formally submit a restart plan for the Kuosheng (No. 2) Nuclear Power Plant to Taiwan's Nuclear Safety Commission before May 17, 2026?
summary: Taipower has not submitted a Kuosheng restart plan as of early May 2026, per the question description. Only 9 days remain until the deadline. Kuosheng faces a major technical prerequisite (fuel rod removal to dry storage) that makes it structurally behind Maanshan in the restart process. Taipower's chairman discussed these complications on May 5 without any hint of imminent plan submission. No news sources suggest a filing is coming in the next 9 days. The only uncertainty comes from the World Nuclear Association loosely stating both plants applied in March 2026, but this appears to be an imprecise summary contradicted by the question's explicit description and Taiwanese news sources. The probability of YES is very low at approximately 3%.

factors.description: Question description explicitly states no Kuosheng plan has been submitted as of early May 2026
Only ~9 days remain until the May 16, 2026 deadline - very short window for formal regulatory submission
Kuosheng restart faces major technical prerequisite: fuel rods must be removed from reactor to dry storage before safety inspections can proceed, delaying any plan submission
Taipower chairman on May 5, 2026 discussed Kuosheng complexities (fuel rod removal, procurement difficulties) without mentioning imminent plan submission
No news reports in either English or Chinese indicate an imminent Kuosheng submission in the next 9 days
World Nuclear Association loosely states both plants applied in March 2026 - possible ambiguity about whether a combined plan was filed that already covers Kuosheng

reasoning:
## Forecast: **3% probability** (YES)

### Summary

This question asks whether Taipower will formally submit a restart plan for the Kuosheng (No. 2) Nuclear Power Plant to Taiwan's Nuclear Safety Commission before May 17, 2026.

**The answer is almost certainly NO.** Key evidence:

1. **No plan has been submitted yet** — The question description explicitly confirms this as of early May 2026
2. **Only ~9 days remain** — An extremely short window for a formal regulatory filing with no public indication one is coming
3. **Major technical blocker** — Kuosheng's reactor core still contains used fuel rods that must be removed to dry storage before safety inspections can proceed; Taipower's chairman highlighted these complications as recently as May 5
4. **No signals of imminent submission** — Extensive search of both English and Chinese news sources found no indication of a Kuosheng plan submission in the near term
5. **Kuosheng lags behind Maanshan** — The government's own timeline puts Kuosheng about a year behind Maanshan (restart target ~2029 vs ~2028)

The only marginal YES scenario involves the World Nuclear Association's loose characterization that "both" plants applied in March 2026, but this is contradicted by the question's explicit statement and detailed Taiwanese reporting confirming only the Maanshan plan was formally filed on March 27.

**Final probability: 0.03 (3%)**
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will Taipower formally submit a restart plan for the Kuosheng (No. 2) Nuclear Power Plant to Taiwan's Nuclear Safety Commission before May 17, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43421)
[Taipower to submit restart plan for 2 nuclear power plants in March 2026 - Focus Taiwan](https://focustaiwan.tw/business/202511280014)
[Lai reveals nuclear power revival plan - Taipei Times](https://www.taipeitimes.com/News/front/archives/2026/03/22/2003854247)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves YES if, before 23:59 Taiwan time on May 16, 2026, Taiwan Power Company (Taipower) has formally submitted a restart plan (operating-period extension/restart application under Article 16-1 of Taiwan's nuclear restart guidelines) for the Kuosheng Nuclear Power Plant (also known as the No. 2 Nuclear Power Plant, 國聖核能發電廠) to Taiwan's Nuclear Safety Commission (NSC, 核能安全委員會). The submission must be confirmed by an official announcement or news release from Taiwan's Ministry of Economic Affairs (https://www.moea.gov.tw/MNS/english/home/English.aspx), the Nuclear Safety Commission (https://www.nusc.gov.tw/), Taipower (https://www.taipower.com.tw/), or [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions). A submission of the Maanshan (No. 3) restart plan, or any preparatory steps that fall short of formally filing the Kuosheng restart plan with the NSC, do not count. Otherwise the question resolves NO.
fine_print: If Taipower submits a single combined restart plan that explicitly covers both Maanshan and Kuosheng, that counts as a Kuosheng submission. A Kuosheng submission that is officially filed with the NSC but later withdrawn or rejected on procedural grounds before May 17, 2026 still counts as YES.

input.anchor: Base rate for a major regulatory filing being submitted within 9 days when the responsible entity has not announced plans to do so and faces known technical blockers: ~3-5%
input.assessment:
This question asks whether Taipower will submit a formal restart plan for Kuosheng to the NSC before May 17, 2026. The question was published today (May 7) and explicitly states no plan has been submitted yet. The deadline is 9 days away. 

Multiple factors make submission extremely unlikely in this timeframe:
1. The question itself confirms no plan has been submitted
2. Kuosheng faces a major blocker: fuel rods must be moved to dry storage first
3. Taipower's own chairman was discussing these complications just 2 days ago without any hint of imminent submission
4. The government's stated timeline has Kuosheng about a year behind Maanshan

The only possible YES scenario is if the WNA's statement that "both" were submitted in March turns out to mean a combined plan was filed. However, the question description explicitly says the Maanshan plan was submitted separately and no Kuosheng plan exists. Since the question author presumably verified this, this interpretation is unlikely.

Another marginal scenario: Taipower could submit a plan even though the technical prerequisites aren't met, as a formal regulatory step while work continues. But nothing in the news suggests this is planned in the next 9 days.
input.calibration_notes: Status quo strongly favors NO. No evidence of imminent submission. The 9-day window is very short for a formal regulatory filing that has not been publicly announced or prepared. Risk of being too generous at even 5%.
input.key_uncertainties: Whether the WNA's characterization of 'both' plans being submitted in March could be literally correct despite the question description saying otherwise. Whether Taipower could surprise with a submission despite no public signals.
input.tool_audit: web_search: returned useful results including recent Chinese-language news (May 2-5, 2026) confirming Kuosheng's technical delays. fetch_url: successfully extracted WNA page content and Taipei Times article. research(): returned background but was limited by training cutoff. Metaculus question data: provided critical context in the description.
input.update_triggers: Official announcement from Taipower, MOEA, or NSC that a Kuosheng restart plan has been filed. News reports of imminent Kuosheng submission.
input.process_reflection: The research tool was limited by its training cutoff (early 2025) and couldn't access live web results. Direct web_search was much more useful. The Chinese-language search results were particularly valuable for understanding the most recent (May 5) statements from Taipower's chairman about Kuosheng's challenges. The question description itself was the most important source, explicitly stating the current status.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
## Verification Summary

**Resolution criteria**: Present and detailed. The agent engaged properly with the specific terms — distinguishing between Maanshan-only submissions, combined plans, and preliminary steps that don't count.

**Factor-by-factor verification against trace:**

1. **Factor 1 (logit=-2.5)**: "No Kuosheng plan submitted as of early May 2026" — Directly grounded in the Metaculus question description which explicitly states: "As of early May 2026, Taipower has not submitted a separate restart plan for the Kuosheng plant." ✓

2. **Factor 2 (logit=-1.5)**: "~9 days remaining" — Factual. Question published May 7, deadline May 16. ✓

3. **Factor 3 (logit=-1.5)**: "Fuel rods must be removed to dry storage first" — Grounded in both the Metaculus description and confirmed by Chinese-language news articles (UDN, Chinatimes) from May 2-5, 2026 found in web searches. The Taipower chairman's May 5 comments explicitly confirm this blocker. ✓

4. **Factor 4 (logit=-1.0)**: "Chairman discussed complexities without mentioning imminent submission" — Supported by the May 5 Chinatimes/UDN article about Chairman Tseng Wen-sheng's comments on nuclear fuel procurement complexity. ✓

5. **Factor 5 (logit=-1.0)**: "No news of imminent Kuosheng submission" — Verified through multiple web searches in both English and Chinese that returned zero results indicating upcoming Kuosheng-specific filing. ✓

6. **Factor 6 (logit=+0.5, confidence=0.3)**: "WNA says both plants applied in March" — Verified against the WNA fetch_url result which returned: "in March 2026 Taipower applied to restart the Maanshan and Kuosheng plants." The agent properly investigated this discrepancy by fetching the Taipei Times article (Lai said both would be submitted "by end of month") and Focus Taiwan (ambiguous singular/plural language about plans), correctly concluding the WNA likely summarizes loosely while the question description is more precise. Appropriately low confidence assigned. ✓

**No hallucinated evidence found.** All factual claims trace to specific tool results.

**No double-counting.** Factors 1 and 5 both concern absence of submission but draw on different evidence sources (question description vs. independent news search). Factors 2-4 address distinct obstacles (time, technical prerequisites, organizational signals).

**No wrong-direction factors.** All five negative-logit factors correctly push toward NO; the one positive-logit factor correctly captures the small YES possibility.

**No contradictory assessment.** Narrative and factors are fully aligned on very low probability.

**Counterargument quality**: Strong. The agent constructs a genuine alternative story centered on the WNA source, the possibility that the question description is outdated, and President Lai's political pressure to submit both plans. This is a real disagreer's argument, not a token gesture. ✓

**Probability assessment (3.0%)**: Appropriate. The evidence overwhelmingly points to NO: explicit confirmation that no plan has been submitted, major technical blockers, only 9 days remaining, no news signals of imminent filing. The 3% appropriately reserves probability mass for the low-confidence WNA scenario (possible combined filing already exists) and tail risk of surprise announcement. My independent estimate would be ~2-5%, consistent with the agent's 3%.

**Confidence in estimate (0.75)**: Reasonable given the WNA ambiguity creates genuine uncertainty about whether a combined plan was already filed.

**Tool audit**: Agent reports no tool failures; ground truth confirms 0 errors across 12 calls. No discrepancy.

**Worldview consistency**: The worldview research entries for Kuosheng are based on pre-2025 training data and don't contradict the agent's findings. No conflicting forecasts found.
